title: Submit job to printer
given: a job file
when: the operator submits the job file using Submission method
then: the printer appends a new controller job to the scheduled jobs AND the controller job is of type Job type
ibdd_representation:
GIVEN JF, SM, CJ, JT [true]
WHEN ?submit.jf,sm [true] JF := jf, SM := sm
THEN !append.cj,sjl [cj = CJ ∧ sjl = SJL ∧ JT = getJobType(SM) ∧ CJ.type = JT] SJL := add(CJ, SJL)
[is_in_list(CJ, SJL) ∧ CJ.type = JT]

title: Complete printing moves job to printed
given: a controller job is in the scheduled jobs AND the controller job is a production job
when: the printer starts printing the controller job AND the printer completes printing the controller job
then: the controller job is in the printed jobs
ibdd_representation:
GIVEN CJ [is_in_list(CJ, SJL) ∧ CJ.type = Production]
WHEN !printstart.cj [cj.id = CJ.id ∧ cj.state = Printing] CJ.state := cj.state
!printcomplete.cj [cj.id = CJ.id ∧ cj.state = Completed] CJ.state := cj.state, PJL := add(CJ, PJL)
THEN [is_in_list(CJ, PJL)]

title: Restart cleanup
given: the printer controller is running
when: the operator restarts the printer controller
then: the printed jobs clean up is executed
ibdd_representation:
GIVEN [is_running(CTRL)]
WHEN ?restart.ctrl [is_running(CTRL)] true
THEN !cleanup.pjl [true] PJL := empty(PJL)
[is_empty(PJL)]

title: Pause printing
given: a controller job is printing
when: the operator pauses the printing of the controller job
then: the controller job is paused
ibdd_representation:
GIVEN CJ [CJ.state = Printing]
WHEN ?pause.cj [cj.id = CJ.id] CJ.state := Paused
THEN [CJ.state = Paused]

title: Resume paused job
given: a controller job is paused
when: the operator resumes printing the controller job
then: the controller job is printing
ibdd_representation:
GIVEN CJ [CJ.state = Paused]
WHEN ?resume.cj [cj.id = CJ.id] CJ.state := Printing
THEN [CJ.state = Printing]

title: Move paused job to waiting
given: a controller job is paused
when: the operator moves the controller job to the waiting jobs before the printer completes printing
then: the controller job is in the waiting jobs AND the controller job is not in the printed jobs
ibdd_representation:
GIVEN CJ [CJ.state = Paused]
WHEN ?move_to_waiting.cj [cj.id = CJ.id ∧ CJ.state = Paused ∧ ¬is_completed(CJ)] CJ := cj, CJ.state := Waiting, WJL := add(CJ, WJL)
THEN [is_in_list(CJ, WJL) ∧ ¬is_in_list(CJ, PJL)]

title: Move from waiting to scheduled
given: a controller job in the waiting jobs
when: the operator moves the controller job to the scheduled jobs
then: the controller job is in the scheduled jobs
ibdd_representation:
GIVEN CJ [is_in_list(CJ, WJL)]
WHEN ?move_to_scheduled.cj [cj.id = CJ.id] WJL := remove(CJ, WJL), SJL := add(CJ, SJL)
THEN [is_in_list(CJ, SJL)]

title: Hard copy output after printing
given: a controller job is in the scheduled jobs
when: the printer starts printing the controller job AND the printer completes printing the controller job
then: there is a printed output AND the printed output is a hard copy of the controller job
ibdd_representation:
GIVEN CJ, PO [is_in_list(CJ, SJL)]
WHEN !printstart.cj [cj.id = CJ.id ∧ cj.state = Printing] CJ.state := cj.state
!printcomplete.cj [cj.id = CJ.id ∧ cj.state = Completed] CJ.state := cj.state
THEN !output.po,cj [po.job_id = CJ.id ∧ po.kind = HardCopy] PO := po
[exists(PO) ∧ PO.job_id = CJ.id ∧ PO.kind = HardCopy]

title: Add two numbers
given: the calculator is ready for use
when: I enter the numbers 5 and 7 and choose addition
then: the calculator displays the result as 12
ibdd_representation:
GIVEN [is_ready(CALC)]
WHEN ?enter.a,b,op [a = 5 ∧ b = 7 ∧ op = Add] A := a, B := b, OP := op
!compute.res [res = add(A, B) ∧ OP = Add] RES := res
THEN [RES = 12]

title: Subtract two numbers
given: the calculator is ready for use
when: I enter the numbers 10 and 3 and choose subtraction
then: the calculator displays the result as 7
ibdd_representation:
GIVEN A, B, R [is_ready(CALC)]
WHEN ?enter_numbers.a,b [a = 10 ∧ b = 3] A := a, B := b
?choose_op.op [op = Subtraction] OP := op, R := A - B
THEN [R = 7]

title: Multiply two numbers
given: the calculator is ready for use
when: I enter the numbers 4 and 6 and choose multiplication
then: the calculator displays the result as 24
ibdd_representation:
GIVEN [is_ready(CALC)]
WHEN ?enter.a,b,op [a = 4 ∧ b = 6 ∧ op = Mul] A := a, B := b, OP := op
!compute.res [A = 4 ∧ B = 6 ∧ OP = Mul ∧ res = A * B] RES := res
THEN [RES = 24]

title: Divide two numbers
given: the calculator is ready for use
when: I enter the numbers 20 and 4 and choose division
then: the calculator displays the result as 5
ibdd_representation:
GIVEN A,B,R [is_ready(CALC)]
WHEN ?enter_numbers.a,b [a = 20 ∧ b = 4] A := a, B := b
?choose_op.op [op = Div] OP := op
THEN !display.r [r = (A / B) ∧ B ≠ 0] R := r
[R = 5]

title: Add three numbers
given: the calculator is ready for use
when: I enter the number 5 and add 3 and then add 2
then: the calculator displays the result as 10
ibdd_representation:
GIVEN [is_ready(CALC)]
WHEN ?enter.n [n = 5] ACC := n
?add.n [n = 3] ACC := ACC + n
?add.n [n = 2] ACC := ACC + n
THEN !display.res [res = ACC] OUT := res
[OUT = 10]

title: Calculate percentage
given: the calculator is ready for use
when: I enter the number 80 and calculate 25 percent
then: the calculator displays the result as 20
ibdd_representation:
GIVEN [is_ready(CALC)]
WHEN ?enter.n [n = 80] N := n
?percent.pct [pct = 25] PCT := pct, RES := (N * PCT) / 100
THEN [display = RES ∧ RES = 20]

title: Square a number
given: the calculator is ready for use
when: I enter the number 9 and choose square operation
then: the calculator displays the result as 81
ibdd_representation:
GIVEN N, RES [is_ready(CALC)]
WHEN ?enter.n [n = 9] N := n
?choose.op [op = Square] true
THEN !display.res [res = N ^ 2] RES := res
[RES = 81]

title: Calculate square root
given: the calculator is ready for use
when: I enter the number 16 and choose square root operation
then: the calculator displays the result as 4
ibdd_representation:
GIVEN [is_ready(CALC)]
WHEN ?enter.n [n = 16] N := n
?choose_op.op [op = Sqrt] OP := op
THEN !display.res [res = sqrt(N)] RES := res
[RES = 4]

title: Chain operations add and multiply
given: the calculator is ready for use
when: I enter 5 add 3 and then multiply by 2
then: the calculator displays the result as 16
ibdd_representation:
GIVEN RES [is_ready(CALC)]
WHEN ?enter_add.a,b [a = 5 ∧ b = 3] RES := a + b
?enter_multiply.res,f [res = RES ∧ f = 2] RES := res * f
THEN [RES = 16]

title: Calculate average of two numbers
given: the calculator is ready for use
when: I enter the numbers 8 and 12 and choose average
then: the calculator displays the result as 10
ibdd_representation:
GIVEN [is_ready(CALC)]
WHEN ?enter.a,b,op [a = 8 ∧ b = 12 ∧ op = Average] A := a, B := b, OP := op
THEN !display.res [res = avg(A, B) ∧ res = 10] RES := res
[RES = 10]

title: Clear and add
given: the calculator displays 42
when: I clear the calculator and then enter 7 and add 3
then: the calculator displays the result as 10
ibdd_representation:
GIVEN [DISP = 42]
WHEN ?clear [true] DISP := 0
?enter.n [n = 7] DISP := n
?add.n [n = 3] DISP := DISP + n
THEN [DISP = 10]

title: Add negative numbers
given: the calculator is ready for use
when: I enter the numbers -5 and -3 and choose addition
then: the calculator displays the result as -8
ibdd_representation:
GIVEN A,B,OP [is_ready(CALC)]
WHEN ?enter.a,b,op [a = -5 ∧ b = -3 ∧ op = Add] A := a, B := b, OP := op
!compute.res [res = add(A,B)] RES := res
THEN [RES = -8]

title: Subtract with negative result
given: the calculator is ready for use
when: I enter the numbers 5 and 12 and choose subtraction
then: the calculator displays the result as -7
ibdd_representation:
GIVEN [is_ready(CALC)]
WHEN ?enter.a,b,op [a = 5 ∧ b = 12 ∧ op = Subtraction] A := a, B := b, OP := op
!compute.res [res = (A - B) ∧ OP = Subtraction] RES := res
THEN [RES = -7]

title: Add decimal numbers
given: the calculator is ready for use
when: I enter the numbers 3.5 and 2.7 and choose addition
then: the calculator displays the result as 6.2
ibdd_representation:
GIVEN [is_ready(CALC)]
WHEN ?enter.a,b,op [a = 3.5 ∧ b = 2.7 ∧ op = Add] A := a, B := b, OP := op
THEN !display.res [res = A + B ∧ OP = Add] RES := res
[RES = 6.2]

title: Multiply by zero
given: the calculator is ready for use
when: I enter the numbers 25 and 0 and choose multiplication
then: the calculator displays the result as 0
ibdd_representation:
GIVEN [is_ready(CALC)]
WHEN ?enter.a,b,op [a = 25 ∧ b = 0 ∧ op = Mul] A := a, B := b, OP := op
THEN !display.res [res = A * B ∧ OP = Mul] RES := res
[RES = 0]

title: Cancel job during printing
given: a controller job is printing
when: the operator cancels the controller job
then: the controller job is removed from scheduled jobs AND the controller job is not in printed jobs AND the printer stops printing
ibdd_representation:
GIVEN CJ [CJ.state = Printing ∧ is_in_list(CJ, SJL)]
WHEN ?cancel.cj [cj.id = CJ.id] CJ.state := Cancelled
THEN !remove.cj,sjl [cj.id = CJ.id ∧ sjl = SJL] SJL := remove(CJ, SJL)
!stopprint.cj [cj.id = CJ.id ∧ CJ.state = Cancelled] CJ.state := Stopped
[¬is_in_list(CJ, SJL) ∧ ¬is_in_list(CJ, PJL) ∧ CJ.state = Stopped]

title: Handle paper jam during production
given: a production job is printing AND the printer has sufficient paper
when: a paper jam occurs AND the operator clears the paper jam AND the operator resumes printing
then: the controller job continues printing from the last successful page
ibdd_representation:
GIVEN CJ [CJ.type = Production ∧ CJ.state = Printing ∧ has_sufficient_paper(PRN) ∧ CJ.last_success_page > 0]
WHEN !paperjam.cj,page [cj.id = CJ.id ∧ page = CJ.last_success_page + 1] CJ.state := Jammed
?clear_jam [true] PRN.jam := false
?resume_print.cj [cj.id = CJ.id ∧ CJ.state = Jammed ∧ PRN.jam = false] CJ.state := Printing
THEN [CJ.state = Printing ∧ CJ.current_page = CJ.last_success_page + 1]

title: Priority job jumps queue
given: there are 3 jobs in the scheduled jobs
when: the operator submits a high priority job AND marks it as urgent
then: the high priority job is inserted at the beginning of scheduled jobs
ibdd_representation:
GIVEN HPJ [size(SJL) = 3]
WHEN ?submit.hpj,sm [hpj.priority = High] HPJ := hpj
?mark_urgent.hpj [hpj.id = HPJ.id] HPJ.urgent := true
THEN !insert_beginning.hpj,sjl [hpj.id = HPJ.id ∧ sjl = SJL] SJL := add_first(HPJ, SJL)
[is_first(HPJ, SJL)]

title: Concurrent pause requests
given: a controller job is printing
when: two operators attempt to pause the same controller job simultaneously
then: the controller job is paused only once AND both operators receive confirmation
ibdd_representation:
GIVEN CJ [CJ.state = Printing]
WHEN ?pause_req.cj,op1 [cj.id = CJ.id ∧ op1.id = OP1.id] true
?pause_req.cj,op2 [cj.id = CJ.id ∧ op2.id = OP2.id] true
THEN !pause.cj [cj.id = CJ.id ∧ CJ.state = Printing] CJ.state := Paused
!pause_confirm.op1,cj [op1.id = OP1.id ∧ cj.id = CJ.id ∧ CJ.state = Paused] true
!pause_confirm.op2,cj [op2.id = OP2.id ∧ cj.id = CJ.id ∧ CJ.state = Paused] true
[CJ.state = Paused]

given: there are 50 jobs in printed jobs AND printed jobs are older than 30 days
when: the scheduled cleanup task runs
then: jobs older than 30 days are removed from printed jobs AND recent jobs remain
ibdd_representation:
GIVEN PJL [size(PJL) = 50 ∧ allOlderThanDays(PJL, 30)]
WHEN !scheduledCleanupRun [true] true
THEN !removeOldJobs.pjl [pjl = PJL] PJL := removeOlderThanDays(PJL, 30)
[allNotOlderThanDays(PJL, 30)]

title: Resume printing after power failure
given: a controller job is printing at page 45 of 100
when: a power failure occurs AND the printer restarts AND the operator resumes the controller job
then: the controller job continues printing from page 45
ibdd_representation:
GIVEN CJ [CJ.state = Printing ∧ CJ.currentPage = 45 ∧ CJ.totalPages = 100]
WHEN !powerfail.cj [cj.id = CJ.id] CJ.state := Paused, CJ.resumePage := CJ.currentPage
!restart.ctrl [is_running(CTRL)] CTRL := CTRL
?resume.cj [cj.id = CJ.id ∧ CJ.state = Paused] CJ.state := Printing, CJ.currentPage := CJ.resumePage
THEN [CJ.state = Printing ∧ CJ.currentPage = 45]

title: Duplicate job submission detection
given: a job file with checksum ABC123 is in scheduled jobs
when: the operator submits the same job file with checksum ABC123
then: the printer rejects the duplicate AND displays a warning message
ibdd_representation:
GIVEN JF [JF.checksum = ABC123 ∧ is_in_list(JF, SJL)]
WHEN ?submit.jf [jf.checksum = ABC123] JF := jf
THEN !reject.jf [jf.checksum = JF.checksum ∧ is_in_list(JF, SJL)] true
!display_warning.msg [msg = DuplicateJob] WARN := msg
[is_in_list(JF, SJL) ∧ WARN = DuplicateJob]
